retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Indications -- FDA-Approved Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The efficacy of fingolimod in treating RRMS has been demonstrated in 3 separate double-blind, randomized, phase III clinical trials. In the placebo-controlled FREEDOMS trial (FTY720 Research Evaluating Effects of Daily Oral Therapy in Multiple Sclerosis), fingolimod demonstrated improved relapse rate, decreased disease disability progression, reduced number of active or enlarging magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) lesions, and brain atrophy over 2 years. The FREEDOMS II trial evaluated fingolimod's safety profile and found that daily administration of 0.5 mg had a favorable benefit-risk ratio when treating RRMS.

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Administration -- Various Therapeutic Approaches of Fingolimod for Relapsing-Remitting Multiple Sclerosis
retrieved_docs.content: Before initiating fingolimod therapy, physicians should rule out disease-modifying therapy–related diseases, such as progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML), infections, tumors, and cardiac pathology. [28]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Fingolimod is contraindicated in patients who have experienced cardiovascular events within the last 6 months, including myocardial infarction, unstable angina, stroke, transient ischemic attack, class III/IV heart failure, or decompensated heart failure requiring hospitalization. Additionally, patients with a history of Mobitz type II heart block, third-degree AV block, or sick sinus syndrome should avoid fingolimod unless they have a working pacemaker. Fingolimod can potentially prolong the QTc interval; therefore, physicians should refrain from initiating fingolimod treatment in patients with a baseline QTc of 500 ms or longer. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the appropriate indications for prescribing fingolimod in patients with multiple sclerosis. Implement a comprehensive monitoring plan to assess the effectiveness and safety of fingolimod treatment in patients with multiple sclerosis. Apply evidence-based guidelines to select the most suitable patients for fingolimod treatment based on individual clinical profiles and disease characteristics. Collaborate with multidisciplinary healthcare professionals, including neurologists, cardiologists, and ophthalmologists, to optimize the management of multiple sclerosis in patients undergoing fingolimod therapy. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Monitoring
retrieved_docs.content: Advise patients to utilize at least 2 effective contraceptive methods during the course of fingolimod therapy and for 2 months following discontinuation of therapy. [2] Educate patients about the risk of developing tumefactive lesions within 6 months of starting fingolimod therapy. [46] Perform a brain MRI scan with and without contrast. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Chronic Transplantation Rejection(Archived) -- Pertinent Studies and Ongoing Trials
retrieved_docs.content: FTY720 (fingolimod) is a novel immunosuppressive agent investigated over the past few decades. FTY has proven highly effective in prolonging graft survival in preclinical models of transplantation and multiple sclerosis. Unlike traditional immunosuppressants, FTY720 is an immunomodulator with a unique mechanism of action, classified as a sphingosine 1-phosphate receptor (S1P-R) modulator. [8] [9] In laboratory animals, activating S1P receptors with fingolimod triggers anti-inflammatory and anti-apoptotic pathways, enhancing myocardial salvage by reducing cardiac fibrosis. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: This activity reviews the indications, mechanism of action, contraindications, potential interactions, and necessary monitoring parameters for fingolimod. This activity also emphasizes the importance of an interdisciplinary healthcare team in optimizing care for patients with RRMS undergoing fingolimod treatment, thereby aiming to enhance patient outcomes through coordinated care.

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Administration -- Discontinuation of Fingolimod
retrieved_docs.content: Discontinuing fingolimod may cause relapse in up to 30% of patients within the first 6 months after discontinuing the therapy. Outcomes are better in patients who initiate a second medication within 2 months of fingolimod therapy. [29] Predictors of disease relapse include a higher relapse rate in the year before discontinuing, female sex, younger age, and a higher Expanded Disability Status scale score. [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Administration -- Various Therapeutic Approaches of Fingolimod for Relapsing-Remitting Multiple Sclerosis
retrieved_docs.content: Fingolimod as first-line therapy for RRMS: Fingolimod has been effective for various conditions, as mentioned below. Fingolimod can be used as first-line therapy for treating clinically isolated syndrome without MRI-positive lesions that meet the dissemination in time according to the McDonald criteria. [26] Fingolimod therapy has also proven effective as a first-line treatment for patients with multiple sclerosis who have a high lesion burden, multiple active lesions on gadolinium-enhancing T1 MRI sequences, multiple relapses in a year, marked progression of deficits, or incomplete recovery from relapses. [2] Fingolimod therapy can be initiated in patients with mild disease progression refractory to other medications, provided they understand the adverse effects of the medication and monitoring requirements. [2] Fingolimod as escalation therapy for RRMS: Fingolimod is considered an escalation therapy for the patient profiles mentioned below.

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: The cardiovascular effect of fingolimod is directly related to S1PR1 activation. Fingolimod binding to S1PR1 on endothelial cells leads to enhanced endothelial cell junction and decreased vascular permeability. This function is dose-dependent, as prolonged fingolimod administration causes vascular injury and increased permeability. Fingolimod causes bradycardia and hypotension by activating endothelial nitric oxide synthase—a Gi-protein–coupled process. [1] Additionally, the binding of fingolimod to S1PR3 leads to neuronal regeneration, bradycardia, arterial vasodilation, and macrophage phagocytosis. [20] [21] [22] [23] Furthermore, the drug may suppress the immune response by downregulating Th17 proliferation and Th17-central memory cells in the peripheral circulation, thereby substantially decreasing the neuroinflammation seen in multiple sclerosis. [24] [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Fingolimod is the first oral disease-modifying agent approved by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for treating relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS). [1] This drug is also indicated for active secondary progressive disease with superimposed relapse and for treating clinically isolated syndromes. [2] [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Monitoring
retrieved_docs.content: After the first dose of fingolimod therapy is administered, various parameters, including vital signs, ECG, and baseline electrolytes, must be monitored. Hourly evaluation of vital signs, especially the patient's heart rate, is essential for up to 6 hours post-initiation due to fingolimod's potential to induce bradycardia. If the patient's heart rate falls below 40 bpm or 30% of the baseline value, or if symptomatic bradycardia develops, therapy should be immediately discontinued by the medical providers. Patients must then undergo prolonged cardiac monitoring for their safety.

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Administration -- Various Therapeutic Approaches of Fingolimod for Relapsing-Remitting Multiple Sclerosis
retrieved_docs.content: Patients who are asymptomatic but have MRI demonstrating gadolinium-enhancing lesions, indicating clinically silent multiple sclerosis, are evaluated for alternative treatment. [2] Patients who experience more than one relapse per year. [2] Patients with more than 9 T2 lesions on MRI at any given time. [2] Fingolimod as de-escalation therapy for RRMS: Fingolimod offers viable options under specific conditions when considering de-escalation strategies for RRMS . Fingolimod therapy may be initiated after discontinuing disease-modifying therapy. Patients should be closely monitored for overlapping immunosuppressant effects. Initiating fingolimod 8 to 12 weeks after natalizumab therapy has been shown to reduce relapse rates compared to glatiramer acetate or no treatment. [2] [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Administration -- Specific Patient Populations
retrieved_docs.content: Renal impairment: Protein binding and half-life of fingolimod in patients with renal impairment are similar to those in healthy subjects. Standard dosing is generally considered safe for patients with mild, moderate, or severe renal impairment, although further studies would be beneficial. [32]

retrieved_docs.title: Fingolimod -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Fingolimod is the first oral disease-modifying agent approved by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for treating multiple sclerosis. The drug effectively manages relapsing and remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS), clinically isolated syndromes, and active secondary progressive disease. The primary function of fingolimod is to inhibit lymphocyte migration into peripheral circulation by antagonizing sphingosine-1-phosphate receptor-1 (S1PR1) functions, thereby reducing the body's inflammatory response. Additionally, fingolimod modulates various immune system activities, including T-cell central and peripheral tolerance, autoreactivity, T-cell differentiation, and regulation of the innate immune system. However, concerns exist regarding fingolimod's impact on the cardiovascular system, particularly cardiac chronotropy, which may elevate mortality risk.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1786
retrieved_docs.content: Tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochlorid may prevent إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, a condition that is classified under the broader category of vascular ischemia. Fingolimod is contraindicated in patients who have إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ. Clinical observations indicate that إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ frequently co‑occurs with خفقانات, and the acute event can evolve into مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد, a chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 3 -- Community 2219
retrieved_docs.content: Fingolimod, including its formulations as fingolimod hydrochloride and fingolimod lauryl sulfate, as well as ketamine hydrochloride, are all contraindicated in patients with فشل قلبي (heart failure). Consequently, these agents should be avoided or used only with extreme caution in individuals diagnosed with heart failure, as their use may exacerbate cardiac dysfunction and compromise patient safety.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1791
retrieved_docs.content: Myocardial infarction (إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ) is a manifestation of vascular ischemia, representing the acute occlusion of heart muscle. Tirofiban and its formulation tirofiban hydrochloride may prevent the occurrence of myocardial infarction by inhibiting platelet aggregation. In contrast, fingolimod hydrochloride is contraindicated in patients with myocardial infarction due to potential adverse effects. Myocardial infarction frequently co‑occurs with palpitations (خفقانات), reflecting arrhythmic complications. It also serves as a precursor to chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease (مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد), indicating a progression from acute to long‑term cardiac pathology. Understanding these interconnections informs therapeutic choices and risk management in cardiovascular care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: Fingolimod, including its formulations as fingolimod hydrochloride and fingolimod lauryl sulfate, is contraindicated for patients with heart failure, indicating that these immunomodulatory agents should be avoided in individuals who have or are at risk for this cardiac condition. Ketamine hydrochloride is similarly contraindicated in the setting of heart failure, reflecting the need for caution when using this anesthetic agent in patients with compromised cardiac function. Conversely, reteplase may be employed to prevent heart failure, suggesting a therapeutic role for this thrombolytic medication in reducing the incidence or severity of cardiac failure when indicated.

retrieved_docs.content: Fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochloride, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, ketamin, and ketamine hydrochloride are all contraindicated in the presence of heart failure, indicating that these agents should be avoided in patients diagnosed with this cardiac condition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 2 -- Community 495
retrieved_docs.content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS is a broad disease category that encompasses إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ (myocardial infarction), which is also classified under vascular ischemia as a form of ischemic heart disease and is inversely related to اعتلال عضلة القلب (cardiomyopathy). Clinical management of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ includes antiplatelet agents such as tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochlorid, whereas disease‑modifying agents fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochlorid and fingolimod lauryl sulfate are contraindicated because of adverse risk. إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ is closely linked to تصلب الشريان التاجي (coronary artery sclerosis), indicating that atherosclerotic changes in the coronary arteries are a major underlying factor. Child conditions of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ include احْتِشاءُ عَضَلِ القَلْبِ الحادّ للجدار الأمامي (acute anterior‑wall myocardial infarction), مرض التاجي (coronary disease), مرض الشريان التاجي (coronary artery disease) and مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد (chronic

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 848
retrieved_docs.content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS represent a more specific subset within the broader condition of فشل قلبي (heart failure), indicating that these cardiac structural and functional abnormalities are closely linked to the development or presence of heart failure. Pharmacologic agents such as fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochloride, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, ketamin, and ketamine hydrochloride are contraindicated in patients with فشل قلبي, meaning they should not be used because they can exacerbate or are unsafe in the setting of heart failure. Conversely, the thrombolytic medication reteplase may be employed to prevent فشل قلبي, suggesting a therapeutic role for reteplase in reducing the risk or severity of heart failure. Understanding these relationships helps clinicians avoid harmful drugs in heart‑failure patients while considering reteplase as a potential preventive strategy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 2 -- Community 378
retrieved_docs.content: Acute coronary insufficiency, referred to by the Arabic term إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, is an acute ischemic heart disease localized to the coronary arteries (věnčité tepny). It is classified under vascular ischemia and serves as a parent node for coronary artery spasm (تشنج الشريان التاجي), coronary artery disease (مرض الشريان التاجي), chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease (مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد), arterial sclerosis in the coronary vessels (تصلب عصيدي في التاجي), and coronary artery grafting procedures (مجازة الشريان التاجي). MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS is also placed under إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ and is linked to heart failure (فشل قلبي). Antiplatelet agents tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochloride may prevent إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, whereas the immunomodulatory drug fingolimod and its formulations (fingolimod hydrochloride, fingolimod lauryl sulfate) are contraindicated in both إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ and فشل قلبي. Fingolimod lauryl sulfate and ketamine hydrochloride are likewise contraindicated in heart failure. The left coronary artery gives rise to the branch věnčité tepny, which are involved in مرض الشريان التاجي, تشنج الشريان التاجي, and تصلب عصيدي في التاجي. Niacinamide (niacinamid) is associated with lipid‑lowering and anti‑arteriosclerotic therapy, is useful for treating elevated triglyceride levels (ارتفاع نسبة الدهون الثلاثية في الدم), and may have a preventive role against مرض الشريان التاجي. This network highlights the anatomical localization of coronary pathologies, their hierarchical relationships, and the therapeutic implications of antiplatelet, immunomodulatory, and lipid‑modifying agents, especially regarding safety in heart failure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 2 -- Community 572
retrieved_docs.content: The community describes a network of ischemic heart disease centered on acute coronary insufficiency (إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ), classified under acute ischemic heart disease, unspecified, and localized to the coronary arteries (věnčité tepny). Vascular ischemia is the parent condition, and the disease gives rise to myocardial, endocardial, pericardial and valve disorders (MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS), which in turn are linked to heart failure (فشل قلبي). Specific downstream coronary manifestations include coronary artery spasm (تشنج الشريان التاجي), coronary artery disease (مرض الشريان التاجي), chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease (مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد), coronary arterial sclerosis (تصلب عصيدي في التاجي), coronary artery inflammation (التهاب الشريان التاجي), and coronary artery rupture. The left coronary artery is composed of branches of the coronary arteries. Therapeutically, antiplatelet agents tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochloride may prevent progression of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, whereas fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochloride, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, ketamine and ketamine hydrochloride are contraindicated in patients with إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ or heart failure. Lipid‑lowering and anti‑arteriosclerosis drugs, exemplified by niacinamide, are associated with management of elevated triglycerides (ارتفاع نسبة الدهون الثلاثية في الدم) and may help prevent coronary artery disease. This integrated hierarchy emphasizes the interdependence of vascular ischemia, its cardiac complications, and the importance of appropriate pharmacologic selection in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1787
retrieved_docs.content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS represent a more specific subset within the broader condition of فشل قلبي (heart failure), indicating that disorders of the myocardium, endocardium, pericardium, and cardiac valves are closely linked to the pathophysiology of heart failure. In clinical practice, the use of fingolimod hydrochlorid, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, and ketamine hydrochloride is contraindicated in patients with فشل قلبي, meaning these medications should be avoided in individuals diagnosed with heart failure to prevent worsening of cardiac function. Recognizing the relationship between these structural cardiac disorders and heart failure, as well as the drug contraindications, is essential for safe patient management and optimal therapeutic decision‑making.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 878
retrieved_docs.content: Myocardial, endocardial, pericardial, and valve disorders are closely linked to heart failure, indicating that these structural cardiac conditions often coexist with or contribute to the development of heart failure. In patients with heart failure, the use of fingolimod, including its hydrochloride and lauryl sulfate formulations, is contraindicated because the drug may exacerbate cardiac dysfunction. Similarly, ketamine hydrochloride is also contraindicated in the setting of heart failure, reflecting the need to avoid medications that can worsen cardiac performance in these patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1490
retrieved_docs.content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS is classified as a subcategory of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ (ischemic heart disease) and also maintains a narrower relationship with فشل قلبي (heart failure), indicating that disorders of the myocardium, endocardium, pericardium, and heart valves often overlap with or progress to heart failure. Vascular ischemia is likewise a parent condition of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, underscoring the role of impaired blood flow in the development of myocardial ischemia. An inverse‑isa relationship links إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ with اعتلال عضلة القلب (cardiomyopathy), reflecting that while both involve cardiac muscle pathology, they represent distinct but related disease entities. إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ frequently co‑occurs with خفقانات (palpitations), and it has a specific child condition termed مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد (chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease), highlighting a more defined clinical presentation within the broader ischemic spectrum. Pharmacologically, tirofiban and its formulation tirofiban hydrochlorid are noted as agents that may prevent إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, suggesting a protective antiplatelet role in reducing myocardial ischemic events. In contrast, fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochlorid, and fingolimod lauryl sulfate are contraindicated in both إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ and فشل قلبي, indicating that use of this immunomodulatory drug could exacerbate ischemic heart disease or heart failure. Additionally, ketamine hydrochloride is contraindicated with فشل قلبي, reflecting the need for caution when employing this anesthetic in patients with compromised cardiac function. Together, these relationships emphasize the interconnected nature of cardiac structural disorders, ischemic pathology, symptomatology, and therapeutic considerations essential for comprehensive patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 612
retrieved_docs.content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS is classified under إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, indicating that these myocardial, endocardial, pericardial and valve disorders are considered a component of ischemic heart disease. Vascular ischemia is also identified as a parent condition of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, underscoring the role of impaired blood flow in the development of this cardiac pathology. Clinical management notes that the antiplatelet agents tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochlorid may prevent إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, suggesting a therapeutic benefit in reducing ischemic events. Conversely, the immunomodulatory drug fingolimod and its formulations (fingolimod hydrochlorid and fingolimod lauryl sulfate) are contraindicated in patients with إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, reflecting a risk of adverse cardiac outcomes. إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ frequently co‑occurs with خفقانات (palpitations) and includes a specific chronic form, مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد, as a child condition. In addition, MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS has a narrower relationship with فشل قلبي (heart failure), indicating that these disorders can progress to or are closely linked with cardiac failure. Accordingly, fingolimod and its related salts, as well as ketamine hydrochloride, are contraindicated in فشل قلبي, highlighting the need to avoid these agents in patients with heart failure. Together, these relationships delineate a network in which ischemic heart disease, its antecedent vascular ischemia, associated symptoms such as palpitations, and downstream complications like heart failure are interconnected, and they define specific pharmacologic agents that may be beneficial or harmful within this clinical context.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 1241
retrieved_docs.content: Myocardial infarction (إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ) is classified within the MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS category and is a child of vascular ischemia. It serves as a parent condition for coronary disease (مرض التاجي) and chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease (مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد). Myocardial infarction is clinically linked to coronary artery atherosclerosis (تصلب الشريان التاجي) and frequently co‑occurs with palpitations (خفقانات). Coronary artery atherosclerosis is associated with a general cardiac disorder (اضطراب قلبي) and heart murmur (نفخة قلبية); its evaluation commonly employs CT coronary angiography (تصوير الأوعية التاجية بالفحص المقطعي المحوسب). Both myocardial infarction and coronary artery atherosclerosis are related to heart failure (فشل قلبي), and the MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS group has a narrower relationship with heart failure. Chest tightness (خُنَاقٌ صَدْرِيّ) is also linked to heart failure. Pharmacologically, tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochloride may prevent myocardial infarction, whereas fingolimod (including its hydrochloride and lauryl sulfate forms) and ketamine hydrochloride are contraindicated in patients with heart failure; fingolimod is additionally contraindicated in myocardial infarction. The specific cardiac disorder “أم دم الشريان التاجي” is a narrow subtype of a general cardiac disorder and is a child of coronary disease (مرض التاجي), representing a particular form of coronary pathology. CT coronary angiography is directly used to diagnose and monitor coronary disease, including its subtypes such as “أم دم الشريان التاجي” and coronary artery atherosclerosis, underscoring the integrated nature of ischemic cardiac conditions, their clinical manifestations, diagnostic imaging, and therapeutic considerations.
